tool_args.label: Fordham Road – Inwood Bus Priority CAB Meeting #3

payload.title: Fordham Road – Inwood Bus Priority
payload.description: Community Advisory Board Meeting #3 presentation on Fordham Road and Inwood bus priority design alternatives

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Metropolitan Transportation Authority, co-presenter of Fordham Road-Inwood Bus Priority project

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)
payload.description: Co-presenter of Fordham Road-Inwood Bus Priority project
MTA program logo shown on presentation

Study of bus priority treatments along the Bx12 corridor, including Fordham Road and 207th Street/University Heights Bridge connection to Inwood, with three design alternatives (A, B, C) under traffic analysis
First SBS route in the Bronx, launched in 2008; busiest bus route in the Bronx and second busiest in NYC
Bx12 local bus route on Fordham Road corridor
Critical crosstown transportation corridor in the Bronx; 85,000 average daily bus riders on 5 routes

tool_args.label: Alternative A: Bus Lanes in Both Directions

payload.description: Existing curbside bus lanes along Fordham Rd would be converted to offset lanes, allowing for more curbside parking and loading zone. Offset or curbside bus lanes will be explored on 207th St, from 10th Ave and through the University Heights Bridge.
payload.location_text: Fordham Road and 207th Street/University Heights Bridge

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road and 207th Street/University Heights Bridge

tool_args.label: Alternative B: Eastbound Busway from Morris Ave to Webster Ave

payload.description: Eastbound through traffic on Fordham Rd would be restricted, except for buses and trucks. Curb access would be allowed for loading/unloading. Bus lanes proposed in Alternative A would remain outside of the Busway section.
payload.location_text: Fordham Road, eastbound from Morris Ave to Webster Ave

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road, eastbound from Morris Ave to Webster Ave

tool_args.label: Alternative C: Busway in Both Directions

payload.description: Both eastbound and westbound through traffic on Fordham Rd would be restricted, except for buses and trucks. Curb access would be allowed for loading/unloading. Bus lanes proposed in Alternative A would remain outside of the Busway section.
Community Advisory Board Meeting #3 for Fordham Road-Inwood Bus Priority project
Continue with traffic analysis to determine feasibility of each scenario
Present findings from traffic analysis and refined designs
Estimated implementation of bus priority treatments

Bx12-SBS slowest from Boston Rd to Broadway westbound

payload.claim_text: Bx12-SBS bus speeds are slowest from Boston Rd to Broadway/Isham St in the westbound direction, and from 207th St/Broadway to Boston Rd in the eastbound direction
payload.subject: Bx12-SBS

tool_args.label: Bus speeds below 4 mph westbound on University Heights Bridge

payload.claim_text: Bus speeds across the University Heights Bridge are some of the slowest segments throughout the Bx12-SBS route. Bus speed averages: Below 4 mph in the westbound direction, 6 – 8 mph in the eastbound direction
payload.subject: University Heights Bridge segment of Bx12-SBS

payload.claim_text: General through traffic will be restricted within Busway parameters. Only buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles will be allowed through access. All vehicles are allowed local access: vehicles are allowed to drive on busway for local trips, pick-up/drop-off, and garage access but must make the next available right turn off Busway.
payload.subject: Busway/Transit & Truck Priority

tool_args.label: Busway benefits: reduced congestion and improved speeds

payload.claim_text: Busway regulations reduce the level of congestion along the Busway corridor. Reduced congestion helps improve bus speeds and reliability and creates more viable options for people to get around.
payload.subject: Busway/Transit & Truck Priority
